text.title: multi-agent-production-bus Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for multi-agent-production-bus as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Context leakage: agents share no memory by default, but accidentally shared global state (e.g. a Python dict) can cause race conditions in async parallel execution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In _parallel_execution, all execute_assignment coroutines run concurrently. Any mutable Python object shared by reference between agent handlers (not passed as message content) is subject to concurrent writes without locking. Keep all inter-agent data in Message objects routed through the bus.

failure-mode.description: Deadlocks in async message-bus designs when agents wait on each other circularly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent A sends to agent B and awaits reply; agent B's handler sends back to agent A before returning. With the default single-event-loop asyncio, this creates a deadlock. Use asyncio.wait_for with timeouts and ensure handlers never await a response from the sender.
